title: Pasing-Obermenzing

infobox.key: [Authority control databases](/wiki/Help:Authority_control "Help:Authority control") [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q255702#identifiers "Edit this at Wikidata")

infobox.value: This [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich") location article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Pasing-Obermenzing&action=edit).

(German pronunciation: [[ˈpaːzɪŋ ʔoːbɐˈmɛntsɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German")) is the 21st borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). It is located west of the city center and has a population of about 81,000.[[1]](#cite_note-population-1) It consists of the two districts [Pasing](/wiki/Pasing "Pasing") and Obermenzing (lit. 'Upper [Menzing](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Menzing "de:Menzing")'), which were both incorporated into Munich in 1938.
["Population December 2023"](https://stadt.muenchen.de/dam/jcr:370fe775-89d2-4a81-b7ca-464ddb514549/jt230102.pdf) (PDF) (in German). Statistisches Amt München. Retrieved 2024-03-20.

[Coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system"): [48°09′11″N 11°27′58″E﻿ / ﻿48.153°N 11.466°E﻿ / 48.153; 11.466](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Pasing-Obermenzing&params=48.153_N_11.466_E_type:city_region:DE)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Borough of Munich, Germany

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5b/M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_21_%28Karte%29_-_Pasing_-_Obermenzing.png/250px-M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_21_%28Karte%29_-_Pasing_-_Obermenzing.png)](/wiki/File:M%C3%BCnchen_-_Stadtbezirk_21_(Karte)_-_Pasing_-_Obermenzing.png)

District map

**Pasing-Obermenzing** (German pronunciation: [[ˈpaːzɪŋ ʔoːbɐˈmɛntsɪŋ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German")) is the 21st borough of [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich"). It is located west of the city center and has a population of about 81,000.[[1]](#cite_note-population-1) It consists of the two districts [Pasing](/wiki/Pasing "Pasing") and Obermenzing (lit. 'Upper [Menzing](https://de.wikipedia.org/wiki/Menzing "de:Menzing")'), which were both incorporated into Munich in 1938.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ed/Bahnhof_M%C3%BCnchen-Pasing.jpg/250px-Bahnhof_M%C3%BCnchen-Pasing.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bahnhof_M%C3%BCnchen-Pasing.jpg)

Pasing train station.
